response.justification: The candidate brings nearly 20 years of broad engineering experience, recent Staff-level work building AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, and genuine high-throughput systems exposure (micro-services handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic). However, Python — a must-have — is absent from the resume entirely, the high-throughput work was in DLang rather than the required stack, and the trajectory shows breadth across product engineering more than sustained distributed-systems ownership or explicit technical-strategy leadership at scale. Worth a conversation given the strong AI-native alignment, but with clear gaps to probe.
strengths: Recent hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP/agentic-workflow tooling experience, matching the AI-native environment
Real high-throughput, low-latency systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/second) with async I/O and caching
Strong craftsmanship signals: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source maintainership, teaching and workshops
concerns: No Python listed anywhere — a stated must-have for the role
Limited evidence of directing technical strategy or cross-functional stakeholder leadership at Staff scope
High-throughput experience is dated (2018–2020) and in DLang; recent roles are product-focused full-stack work
